article.body_preview:
DHAKA - Bangladesh Bank (BB) today announced 'cautiously accommodative' Monetary Policy Statement (MPS) for the second half of the current fiscal year 2022-23 (H2FY23).

"BB's monetary and credit programs for H2FY23 will pursue a cautiously accommodative policy stance to contain inflationary and exchange rate pressures, support desired economic growth, ensuring the necessary flow of funds to the economy's productive and employment generating activities," said BB Governor Abdur Rouf Talukder whil

llm_connections.evidence_quote: pursue a cautiously accommodative policy stance to contain inflationary and exchange rate pressures
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article states the central bank's policy will 'contain ... exchange rate pressures' at the national level, matching the feature's country-level exchange rate volatility scope.

verification.quote_best_match: pursue a cautiously accommodative policy stance to contain inflationary and exchange rate pressures,
verification.judge_reasoning: The article explicitly says BB's programs 'will pursue a cautiously accommodative policy stance to contain ... exchange rate pressures,' which states a clear cause-effect (policy -> containing exchange rate pressures) and refers to national-level (Bangladesh) exchange rate conditions.

llm_connections.evidence_quote: promote import-substituting economic activities and dissuade imports of non-essential commodities to reduce the exchange rate pressure
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article links discouraging imports of non-essential goods to reducing 'exchange rate pressure' for the country, which aligns with the feature's focus on national exchange rate volatility.

verification.quote_best_match: promote import-substituting economic activities and dissuade imports of non-essential commodities to reduce the exchange rate pressure,
verification.judge_reasoning: The article contains the sentence stating BB will 'promote import-substituting economic activities and dissuade imports of non-essential commodities to reduce the exchange rate pressure,' which explicitly expresses a causal/intentional link at the national (Bangladesh) level between discouraging non-essential imports and lowering exchange rate pressure.
